message:
test(search): epic completion — parity regression suite (bd-srch-6)

Final sub-bead of bd-srch-epic. Adds 4 regression tests to lock in
the LLRB+core-port behavior:

- fl_tsearch_descending_inserts_balanced: 1024 descending keys then
  tfind all + tdestroy (unbalanced BST would chain to depth 1024;
  LLRB stays O(log n))
- fl_tsearch_random_then_delete_half_stays_balanced: 512 deterministic
  xorshift keys, delete every other, verify survivors via tfind
- fl_lsearch_then_lfind_consistency: 5 keys via lsearch + readback
  via lfind
- fl_hsearch_capacity_full_returns_null: small-table fill + extra
  ENTER probe

All 14 search conformance tests pass + 24 core unit tests
(12 rb_tree + 5 hash + 7 linear) = 38 tests total for the family.

Closes bd-srch-6 and bd-srch-epic. Family now 100% native Rust:
- core/search/rb_tree.rs    LLRB, 332 lines + 12 tests
- core/search/hash.rs        LinearProbeTable, 184 lines + 5 tests
- core/search/linear.rs      lfind/lsearch helpers, 116 lines + 7 tests
- abi/search_abi.rs          reduced to thin layout-compat wrappers

The previous abi-only impl had:
- Unbalanced BST (O(n) worst case)
- No tdestroy
- Hash table + linear search inlined in abi (no unit tests
  independent of the FFI surface)

Now full glibc parity for the search.h family with O(log n) tree
operations and the GNU tdestroy extension.
